problem_statement: Find the first occurrence position of a pattern string inside a larger string using right-to-left matching and skip tables.

reproduction_targets.target: Exact string search with Boyer-Moore right-to-left comparisons and delta-style shifts
reproduction_targets.success_criteria: Matches a reference substring search on fixed and randomized exact-match cases.

evidence.quote: searches for the location... of the first occurrence

reproduction_targets.success_criteria: Average references per character passed are below 1 for length-5 English-like and 100-character-alphabet patterns, and generally decrease as pattern length increases.

evidence.quote: references to string per character passed is less than 1

language_hints: PDP-10 assembly language implementation mentioned; reproduction uses Python

evidence.quote: pat is a string of length patlen

outputs.name: match position
outputs.description: First occurrence position; all-match wrapper used for correctness tests

steps.name: Preprocess bad-character table
steps.description: Record rightmost occurrences to derive delta1 shifts.
steps.formula: delta1(char)=patlen if char absent else patlen-j for rightmost j

evidence.quote: The delta1 table has an entry for each character

steps.name: Preprocess good-suffix table
steps.description: Compute shifts for plausible reoccurrences of matched suffixes.

evidence.quote: Thus we define delta2 as follows

steps.name: Search from right to left
steps.description: Align pattern, compare from final character backward, and shift by max bad-character/good-suffix shift after mismatch.

datasets.name: Three source strings from Section 6
datasets.substitute_strategy: Use deterministic binary random, extracted-paper English text, and 100-character random sources.

evidence.quote: three different kinds of source strings

metrics.name: references per character passed
metrics.formula: string character references / characters passed before match or exhaustion

evidence.quote: English pattern of length 5... inspects 0.24 characters

metrics.name: match correctness
metrics.formula: algorithm output equals reference substring search

evidence.quote: If... returns a number, then it is the position

baselines.name: Knuth-Morris-Pratt
baselines.reported_result: References string precisely 1 time per character passed

baselines.name: Simple search
baselines.reported_result: About 1.1 references per character on English sample

evidence.quote: simple search algorithm references string about 1.1 times

experiments.name: Section 6 synthetic analogue
experiments.purpose: Regenerate the references-per-character-passed trend for three source types.

evidence.quote: averaged these measures across all 300 samples
Our search algorithm may be specified
300 patterns... for each patlen from 1 to 14

ambiguities.item: Original exact corpora and random samples

ambiguities.impact: Paper uses one-based; Python uses zero-based

missing_but_required.impact: Exact empirical values cannot be reproduced
